tasks.title: Fix ISR exclude patterns with optional locale prefix in astro.config.mjs
tasks.description:
Update the 8 ISR exclude regex patterns in `apps/web/astro.config.mjs` to include the optional locale prefix `(\/(?:en|pt))?` at the start of each pattern, so that auth/mi-cuenta pages in en/pt locales are properly excluded from ISR cache.

Affected file: `apps/web/astro.config.mjs` (lines 69-76).

Key changes:
- `/^\/mi-cuenta(.*)$/` → `/^(\/(?:en|pt))?\/mi-cuenta(.*)?$/`
- `/^\/auth(.*)$/` → `/^(\/(?:en|pt))?\/auth(.*)?$/`
- All 8 patterns get the optional locale prefix
- Note: Spanish (default locale) uses no prefix in Astro, so `(\/(en|pt))?` is correct (not `(\/(es|en|pt))?`)
- Also ensure index pages of alojamientos/eventos are excluded (fix GAP-126: `(.*)` → `(.*)?`)
- Also fix tipo/categoria patterns to include parent path prefix (GAP-127)

Acceptance criteria:
- Auth pages in /en/auth/* and /pt/auth/* are excluded from ISR cache
- /es/auth/* continues to be excluded
- detail pages of alojamientos and eventos are NOT excluded (they must be ISR-cached)
- listing index pages /alojamientos/ and /eventos/ are excluded
- tipo and categoria pages have correct path-specific exclusion

Tests: Create `apps/web/test/isr-exclude-patterns.test.ts` that imports the regex patterns and asserts inclusion/exclusion for all URL categories (resolves GAP-062).

subtasks.title: Update 8 regex patterns with optional locale prefix
Fix index page patterns (GAP-126)
Fix tipo/categoria patterns with parent path prefix (GAP-127)
Create isr-exclude-patterns.test.ts with full URL matrix

tasks.title: Add page-revalidation cron to apps/api/vercel.json
tasks.description:
The `page-revalidation` cron job exists in the registry but is not registered in `apps/api/vercel.json`. Without this entry, Vercel Cron never triggers the job in production.

Affected file: `apps/api/vercel.json`.

Key change: Add `{ "path": "/api/v1/cron/page-revalidation", "schedule": "0 * * * *" }` to the `crons` array.

Acceptance criteria:
- Entry exists in vercel.json crons array
- Schedule matches the existing 6 other cron jobs pattern (hourly: `0 * * * *`)
- Path matches the actual mounted route of the cron job

Resolves: GAP-073, GAP-081, GAP-085.

subtasks.title: Verify actual cron route path in registry
Add cron entry to vercel.json

tasks.title: Add revalidation entry to admin sidebar menu with i18n keys
tasks.description:
The revalidation dashboard exists at `/revalidation/` but is not discoverable from the sidebar — there is no menu item. Admins can only access it via direct URL.

Affected files:
- `apps/admin/src/lib/menu.ts`: add entry in the administration section
- `packages/i18n/src/locales/es/admin-menu.json`: add `admin-menu.admin.revalidation` key
- `packages/i18n/src/locales/en/admin-menu.json`: add same key in English
- `packages/i18n/src/locales/pt/admin-menu.json`: add same key in Portuguese

Key change: Add `{ titleKey: 'admin-menu.admin.revalidation', to: '/revalidation', permission: PermissionEnum.REVALIDATION_CONFIG_VIEW }` to the menu tree.

Acceptance criteria:
- Menu item appears for users with REVALIDATION_CONFIG_VIEW permission
- Menu item is hidden for users without the permission
- i18n keys present in all 3 locales

Resolves: GAP-134.

subtasks.title: Add menu item to apps/admin/src/lib/menu.ts
Add i18n key in es/en/pt admin-menu.json files

tasks.title: Add logger.warn when NoOp adapter is used in non-local environments
tasks.description:
The adapter factory silently returns NoOpRevalidationAdapter when HOSPEDA_REVALIDATION_SECRET is missing, even in production/staging. This means ISR revalidation is silently disabled with no visible warning.

Affected file: `packages/service-core/src/revalidation/adapters/adapter-factory.ts`.

Key change: Before `return new NoOpRevalidationAdapter()`, check if the environment is non-local (production or staging). If so, call `logger.warn('ISR revalidation DISABLED: missing HOSPEDA_REVALIDATION_SECRET. On-demand page revalidation will not work.')` using `@repo/logger`.

Acceptance criteria:
- Warning logged when NoOp used in production
- No warning in local/test/development environments
- Warning message is actionable (mentions the env var name)

Resolves: GAP-129.

subtasks.title: Add environment check in adapter factory
Add logger.warn call with descriptive message

tasks.title: Add trigger parameter to revalidateByEntityType method
tasks.description:
The `revalidateByEntityType` method in `RevalidationService` hardcodes `trigger='cron'` even when called from manual revalidation endpoints. This corrupts the audit trail — manual revalidations appear as cron-triggered in logs.

Affected file: `packages/service-core/src/revalidation/revalidation.service.ts`.

Key change: Add `trigger: RevalidationTrigger` parameter to `revalidateByEntityType`. The POST /revalidate/type route passes `'manual'` and the cron job passes `'cron'`.

Acceptance criteria:
- `revalidateByEntityType` accepts optional `trigger` parameter (default: `'cron'` for backward compat)
- Manual API route passes `trigger: 'manual'`
- Cron job continues to pass `trigger: 'cron'` (or relies on default)
- Logs correctly show trigger source

Resolves: GAP-046, GAP-014 partial.

subtasks.title: Add trigger param to revalidateByEntityType signature
Update POST /revalidate/type route to pass trigger='manual'
Update cron job to pass trigger='cron'

tasks.title: Pass triggeredBy and reason to service in manual, entity and type revalidation routes
tasks.description:
The manual and entity/type revalidation routes do not pass `triggeredBy` (userId of the actor) or `reason` to the service, so the audit trail is missing attribution.

Affected files:
- `apps/api/src/routes/revalidation/index.ts`: POST /revalidate/manual, POST /revalidate/entity, POST /revalidate/type handlers

Key changes:
- In each handler, extract actor with `getActorFromContext(c)` (the manual route may already do this)
- Pass `triggeredBy: actor?.id` and `reason` to `revalidatePaths` / `revalidateByEntityType`

Acceptance criteria:
- All 3 POST revalidation routes extract actor from context
- triggeredBy is stored in revalidation_log for manual triggers
- reason field is propagated correctly

Resolves: GAP-047, GAP-131.

subtasks.title: Extract actor in POST /revalidate/entity handler
Extract actor in POST /revalidate/type handler
Verify POST /revalidate/manual already passes actor

tasks.title: Add beforeLoad permission guard to admin revalidation route
tasks.description:
The admin revalidation page `/_authed/revalidation/` is accessible to any authenticated user, including owners without REVALIDATION_* permissions. The sidebar hides the link but the route itself has no guard.

Affected file: `apps/admin/src/routes/_authed/revalidation/index.tsx`.

Key change: Add `beforeLoad` handler in `createFileRoute` that checks `REVALIDATION_CONFIG_VIEW` permission, following the same pattern as other protected admin routes (e.g., redirect with 403 or throw notFound).

Acceptance criteria:
- Users without REVALIDATION_CONFIG_VIEW get redirected/rejected on direct URL access
- Users with the permission can access normally
- Pattern matches existing admin route guards

Resolves: GAP-070, GAP-088.

subtasks.title: Add beforeLoad with REVALIDATION_CONFIG_VIEW check
Verify redirect pattern matches other admin routes

tasks.title: Remove prerender=true from auth/forgot-password.astro
tasks.description:
The `apps/web/src/pages/[lang]/auth/forgot-password.astro` page has `export const prerender = true`, making it a static HTML page built at build time. Auth pages must be SSR to handle dynamic tokens, error messages, and redirects correctly.

Affected file: `apps/web/src/pages/[lang]/auth/forgot-password.astro`.

Key change: Remove `export const prerender = true` (line 2).

Acceptance criteria:
- Page renders server-side on every request
- No `prerender = true` or `getStaticPaths` in auth pages
- Combined with T-001 (ISR exclude fix), auth pages are properly excluded from ISR cache in en/pt

Resolves: GAP-084.

subtasks.title: Remove prerender = true from forgot-password.astro
Verify page still works without static generation

tasks.title: Document API route URL divergence as technical decision in spec
tasks.description:
The implementation uses sub-path routes (`/revalidate/manual`, PATCH with `:id` UUID) that differ from the spec's proposed routes (PUT with `:entityType`). The current implementation is more RESTful and consistent with project conventions, so the spec was misaligned, not the code.

Affected file: `.claude/specs/SPEC-034-on-demand-isr-revalidation/spec.md` or equivalent spec document.

Key change: Add a section documenting the deliberate divergence: PATCH with UUID primary key is more RESTful for partial updates; hyphens in sub-paths are not used in this project. No code changes required.

Acceptance criteria:
- Divergence documented as explicit technical decision
- No code changes to API routes
- Future developers understand why the route structure differs from original spec

Resolves: GAP-049, GAP-050, GAP-132.

subtasks.title: Add technical decision note to spec documentation

tasks.title: Align RevalidationService and adapter to RO-RO pattern with corrected return types
tasks.description:
Multiple methods in RevalidationService and the adapter interface violate the project's mandatory RO-RO pattern and have void return types where the spec requires ReadonlyArray<RevalidationResult>.

Affected files:
- `packages/service-core/src/revalidation/revalidation.service.ts`
- `packages/service-core/src/revalidation/adapters/revalidation.adapter.ts`
- `packages/service-core/src/revalidation/adapters/vercel-revalidation.adapter.ts`
- `packages/service-core/src/revalidation/adapters/noop-revalidation.adapter.ts`
- All service hooks (~20-30 call sites) in the 8 service files

Key changes:
- `revalidatePaths(paths[], triggeredBy?, reason?, trigger?)` → `revalidatePaths({ paths, reason, triggeredBy, trigger? })` returning `ReadonlyArray<RevalidationResult>`
- `revalidateByEntityType(entityType)` → `revalidateByEntityType({ entityType, trigger? })` returning `ReadonlyArray<RevalidationResult>`
- `adapter.revalidate(path)` → `adapter.revalidate({ path })` returning `RevalidationResult`
- `adapter.revalidateMany(paths[])` → `adapter.revalidateMany({ paths })` returning `ReadonlyArray<RevalidationResult>`
- Update all ~20-30 call sites in service hooks to use new signatures
- Rename `RevalidatePathResult` type to `RevalidationResult` for consistency with spec

Acceptance criteria:
- All public methods use RO-RO object params
- Return types are ReadonlyArray<RevalidationResult> where specified
- All call sites updated (no TypeScript errors)
- Existing unit tests pass with updated call signatures

Resolves: GAP-064, GAP-065, GAP-080.

subtasks.title: Update adapter interface to RO-RO
Update VercelRevalidationAdapter and NoOpAdapter to RO-RO
Update revalidatePaths and revalidateByEntityType in service
Update all call sites in 8 service hooks
Rename RevalidatePathResult to RevalidationResult
Fix TypeScript errors and run typecheck

tasks.title: Add locales, maxCronRevalidations and logRetentionDays to RevalidationServiceConfig
tasks.description:
RevalidationServiceConfig and InitRevalidationParams are missing `locales`, `maxCronRevalidations`, and `logRetentionDays` fields. These are currently hardcoded (5s/30s debounce default, 30d log retention). This also blocks GAP-043 (entityResolver) which needs the locales list to generate multi-locale paths.

Affected files:
- `packages/service-core/src/revalidation/revalidation.service.ts` (config interface, lines 9-18)
- `packages/service-core/src/revalidation/revalidation-init.ts` (init params, lines 11-30)
- `apps/api/src/index.ts` (initialization call site)

Key changes:
- Add `locales: ReadonlyArray<string>` to config (default from `@repo/i18n` SUPPORTED_LOCALES)
- Add `maxCronRevalidations: number` (default 500)
- Add `logRetentionDays: number` (default 30)
- Expose via getters so cron reads from config instead of hardcoded values
- In `initializeRevalidationService`, pass SUPPORTED_LOCALES from `@repo/i18n`

Acceptance criteria:
- Config interface has all 3 new fields with defaults
- Cron job reads logRetentionDays from service config
- Initialization passes SUPPORTED_LOCALES
- Existing behavior unchanged when defaults are used

Resolves: GAP-048, GAP-068.

subtasks.title: Add locales, maxCronRevalidations, logRetentionDays to config interface
Add getters on RevalidationService
Pass SUPPORTED_LOCALES in initializeRevalidationService
Update cron job to read logRetentionDays from service config

tasks.title: Thread entityType through revalidatePaths to writeLog call chain
tasks.description:
The `writeLog` method in RevalidationService always receives `entityType: 'unknown'` because the entityType is not threaded through the call chain from `scheduleRevalidation` → `debouncePaths` → `revalidatePaths` → `writeLog`. This makes the audit trail completely useless for diagnostics.

Affected file: `packages/service-core/src/revalidation/revalidation.service.ts`.

Key changes:
- Identify where entityType is available (in the EntityChangeData passed to scheduleRevalidation)
- Thread entityType from scheduleRevalidation through debouncePaths and revalidatePaths to writeLog
- The two hardcoded `entityType: 'unknown'` call sites (lines ~120 and ~185) must be replaced with the actual entityType from the event context

Acceptance criteria:
- Revalidation logs in DB have the correct entityType (not 'unknown')
- All 3 triggers (hook, manual, cron) log entityType correctly
- Unit tests verify entityType is persisted in logs

Resolves: GAP-044, GAP-079.

subtasks.title: Trace entityType flow from scheduleRevalidation to writeLog
Add entityType param to revalidatePaths internal method
Replace hardcoded 'unknown' with actual entityType
Add test asserting correct entityType in logs

tasks.title: Change debounce key to entityType:entityId and update default from 5000 to 30000
tasks.description:
The debounce in RevalidationService uses the individual path as the key (creating one timer per path), instead of `entityType:entityId` (one timer per entity). This means 15 paths for one entity create 15 independent timers instead of 1, defeating the purpose of debouncing.

Additionally, the default debounce is 5000ms but spec requires 30000ms.

Affected file: `packages/service-core/src/revalidation/revalidation.service.ts`.

Key changes:
- Change debounce key from `path` to `${entityType}:${entityId}` (when entityId available, fallback to entityType)
- Collect all paths for the same entity key, batch them when the timer fires
- Change default `debounceMs` from `5000` to `30000`

Note: This task depends on T-010 (RO-RO) because the new entityId field needs to come from EntityChangeEvent. If entityId is not yet available in EntityChangeData, use entityType as the key until T-019 adds the full EntityChangeEvent wrapper.

Acceptance criteria:
- One debounce timer per entity (not per path)
- Default debounce is 30 seconds
- Rapid successive updates to the same entity result in one batch revalidation
- Unit tests verify debounce behavior with entity-keyed batching

Resolves: GAP-045, GAP-091, GAP-020.

subtasks.title: Change debounce key from path to entityType:entityId
Accumulate paths per entity key until timer fires
Change default debounceMs from 5000 to 30000
Update unit tests for debounce batching behavior

tasks.title: Resolve parent slug in AccommodationReview and DestinationReview service hooks
tasks.description:
Both review services call `scheduleRevalidation({ entityType: 'accommodation_review' })` without passing the parent entity's slug. The entity-path-mapper requires `accommodationSlug` / `destinationSlug` to generate detail page paths. Without it, only the listing page is revalidated and the accommodation/destination detail page (which shows the review) is never refreshed.

For DestinationReviewService this is even more severe: the path mapper returns an empty array when `destinationSlug` is undefined, meaning ZERO pages are revalidated for destination review events.

Affected files:
- `packages/service-core/src/services/accommodationReview/accommodationReview.service.ts`
- `packages/service-core/src/services/destinationReview/destinationReview.service.ts`

Key changes:
- In each CRUD hook (_afterCreate, _afterUpdate, _afterSoftDelete, _afterHardDelete, _afterRestore), look up the parent entity slug from `entity.accommodationId` / `entity.destinationId` via DB model query
- Pass the resolved slug as `accommodationSlug` / `destinationSlug` in the scheduleRevalidation call
- Follow the existing pattern used in AccommodationService for resolving related data before revalidation

Acceptance criteria:
- AccommodationReview hooks pass accommodationSlug in all 5 CRUD hooks
- DestinationReview hooks pass destinationSlug in all 5 CRUD hooks
- Accommodation/destination detail pages are revalidated when their reviews change
- Unit tests verify parent slug is resolved and passed

Resolves: GAP-066, GAP-082, GAP-083.

subtasks.title: Add parent slug lookup in AccommodationReviewService hooks
Add parent slug lookup in DestinationReviewService hooks
Add unit tests for parent slug resolution

tasks.title: Add _afterUpdateVisibility hook with revalidation to all 8 services
tasks.description:
None of the 8 services implement `_afterUpdateVisibility`. When a content item is published or unpublished (visibility toggled), the ISR cache is not invalidated. Published content doesn't appear; hidden content remains visible until the next cron cycle.

Affected files (8 services):
- `packages/service-core/src/services/accommodation/accommodation.service.ts`
- `packages/service-core/src/services/destination/destination.service.ts`
- `packages/service-core/src/services/event/event.service.ts`
- `packages/service-core/src/services/post/post.service.ts`
- `packages/service-core/src/services/tag/tag.service.ts`
- `packages/service-core/src/services/amenity/amenity.service.ts`
- `packages/service-core/src/services/accommodationReview/accommodationReview.service.ts`
- `packages/service-core/src/services/destinationReview/destinationReview.service.ts`

Key changes: Implement `_afterUpdateVisibility` in each service with the same fire-and-forget pattern as `_afterUpdate`, passing full EntityChangeData context.

Acceptance criteria:
- All 8 services have _afterUpdateVisibility with revalidation call
- Pattern is consistent with _afterUpdate hook in each service
- Entity slug and relevant context is passed (same as _afterUpdate)
- Unit tests cover the visibility hook in at least 2 representative services

Resolves: GAP-076 (A6).

subtasks.title: Implement _afterUpdateVisibility in accommodation service
Implement _afterUpdateVisibility in destination service
Implement _afterUpdateVisibility in event service
Implement _afterUpdateVisibility in post service
Implement _afterUpdateVisibility in tag, amenity, accommodationReview, destinationReview services
Add unit tests for visibility hook in 2 representative services

tasks.title: Pass slug context in _afterSoftDelete and _afterHardDelete hooks across all services
tasks.description:
The `_afterSoftDelete` and `_afterHardDelete` hooks in most services pass only `{ entityType }` to `scheduleRevalidation`, losing the slug. When an entity is deleted, only the listing page gets revalidated. The detail page (which should show 404) keeps serving stale cached content until the cache expires (up to 24h).

Affected files: all 8 service files (same as T-015).

Key changes: In `_afterSoftDelete` and `_afterHardDelete`, pass `slug: entity.slug` (and `destinationSlug`, `accommodationType`, etc. where applicable) — the entity object is available as the hook parameter and contains all needed data.

Also wrap each `scheduleRevalidation` call in try-catch (without rethrowing) with a `logger.warn` to prevent revalidation errors from propagating and blocking the CRUD operation. This applies to all 7 services that currently lack try-catch (only accommodation service has it).

Acceptance criteria:
- Slug is passed in soft/hard delete hooks in all 8 services
- Detail page paths are included in revalidation after deletion
- All 8 services have try-catch around scheduleRevalidation calls
- No CRUD operation can be blocked by a revalidation error

Resolves: GAP-077 (A6), GAP-078 (A6).

subtasks.title: Add slug context to _afterSoftDelete and _afterHardDelete in all 8 services
Add try-catch around scheduleRevalidation in 7 services that lack it

tasks.title: Implement entityResolver pattern for revalidateByEntityType with DB queries
tasks.description:
The `revalidateByEntityType` method generates only generic listing paths because it cannot query the DB for individual entities (service-core has no DB dependency). The spec requires querying all published entities of the type and generating full detail paths.

Solution: Inject an `entityResolver` interface into the service during initialization. The API layer provides the concrete implementation using real models.

Affected files:
- `packages/service-core/src/revalidation/revalidation.service.ts`: add entityResolver field and use it in revalidateByEntityType
- `packages/service-core/src/revalidation/revalidation-init.ts`: add entityResolver to InitRevalidationParams
- `packages/service-core/src/revalidation/entity-resolver.types.ts` (new file): define EntityResolver interface
- `apps/api/src/index.ts` or adjacent file: implement EntityResolver with real model queries for each entity type

Key changes:
- Define interface: `EntityResolver { resolveByType(entityType: RevalidationEntityType): Promise<ReadonlyArray<EntityChangeData>> }`
- In `initializeRevalidationService`, accept optional `entityResolver` parameter
- In `revalidateByEntityType`, if resolver available, use it to fetch entities and generate detail paths; otherwise fall back to listing-only behavior
- The concrete resolver in apps/api queries accommodation/destination/event/post/tag models for published entities, extracting slugs and relevant context

Acceptance criteria:
- entityResolver interface defined and exported from service-core
- Service uses resolver when available, falls back gracefully when not
- API provides concrete resolver implementation with DB queries for all 8 entity types
- Cron job revalidates detail pages for all published entities
- Unit tests mock the resolver and verify path generation

Resolves: GAP-043, GAP-005 partial.

subtasks.title: Define EntityResolver interface in entity-resolver.types.ts
Add entityResolver to service and InitRevalidationParams
Implement resolver usage in revalidateByEntityType
Implement concrete EntityResolver in apps/api with model queries
Wire concrete resolver in initializeRevalidationService call
Add unit tests with mocked resolver

tasks.title: Fix POST /revalidate/entity to lookup entity by ID and use actual slug
tasks.description:
POST /revalidate/entity receives `entityType` and `entityId` in the request body but completely ignores `entityId`. It calls `revalidateByEntityType(entityType)`, which is identical to POST /revalidate/type. The endpoint should revalidate ONLY the specific entity identified by entityId.

Affected file: `apps/api/src/routes/revalidation/index.ts` (entity route handler).

Key changes:
- Use the entityResolver (from T-017) to fetch a single entity by type+id
- Construct EntityChangeData with the entity's real slug and context fields
- Call `scheduleRevalidation` with the specific entity event (not revalidateByEntityType)
- Return proper 404 if entity not found by the given id

Acceptance criteria:
- POST /revalidate/entity revalidates ONLY the specified entity's paths
- Returns 404 with specific error message if entity not found
- Returns results array showing which paths were revalidated
- Does not fall back to full-type revalidation

Resolves: GAP-003, GAP-004 partial.

subtasks.title: Implement entity lookup by type+id in route handler
Build EntityChangeData from fetched entity
Call scheduleRevalidation with specific entity event
Add 404 response for entity not found
Add integration tests: valid entity, unknown entity, 401/403

tasks.title: Implement VercelAdapter with AbortController timeout, chunked batching and token validation
tasks.description:
The VercelRevalidationAdapter is missing three spec-required features: timeout protection, chunked batch processing, and constructor token validation.

Affected file: `packages/service-core/src/revalidation/adapters/vercel-revalidation.adapter.ts`.

Key changes:
1. **Token validation**: In constructor, if `config.bypassToken?.trim()` is empty, throw descriptive error
2. **AbortController timeout**: Wrap each `fetch` in a 10-second AbortController signal: `const controller = new AbortController(); setTimeout(() => controller.abort(), 10000);`
3. **Chunked batching in revalidateMany**: Split `paths` into chunks of 10, `await Promise.allSettled(chunk)`, delay 200ms between chunks using `await new Promise(r => setTimeout(r, 200))`

Acceptance criteria:
- Constructor throws if bypassToken is empty/whitespace
- Each revalidation request has a 10s timeout (aborts on exceeded)
- revalidateMany processes at most 10 paths in parallel at a time, with 200ms inter-chunk delay
- Timeout results in the path being reported as failed (not throwing)
- Unit tests cover: token validation, timeout scenario, chunked batching, partial success

Resolves: GAP-037, GAP-060, GAP-090, GAP-128, GAP-012.

subtasks.title: Add bypassToken validation in constructor
Add AbortController with 10s timeout to revalidate()
Implement chunked batching (10 paths, 200ms delay) in revalidateMany()
Add unit tests: token validation, timeout, batching, partial success

tasks.title: Fix RevalidateEntityButton: useTranslations, permission check, className prop
tasks.description:
The RevalidateEntityButton component has 3 issues:
1. Hardcodes Spanish strings in toasts instead of using i18n
2. Missing permission check for REVALIDATION_TRIGGER (should return null if user lacks permission)
3. Missing `className` prop that the spec defines

Affected file: `apps/admin/src/components/RevalidateEntityButton.tsx`.

Key changes:
- Import and call `useTranslations()` hook
- Replace 4 hardcoded Spanish strings with `t('revalidation.messages.revalidateSuccess')`, `t('revalidation.messages.revalidateError')`, `t('revalidation.actions.revalidate')`, etc.
- Add `useHasAnyPermission([PermissionEnum.REVALIDATION_TRIGGER])` and `if (!hasPermission) return null`
- Add `className?: string` to props interface and pass it to the underlying Button component

Acceptance criteria:
- No hardcoded Spanish strings in the component
- Component returns null for users without REVALIDATION_TRIGGER
- className prop is accepted and applied to the button
- All 3 supported locales (es/en/pt) display correct text

Resolves: GAP-032, GAP-033, GAP-057, GAP-135.

subtasks.title: Add useTranslations and replace hardcoded strings
Add REVALIDATION_TRIGGER permission check
Add className prop to interface and Button usage

tasks.title: Integrate RevalidateEntityButton in 4 entity edit pages
tasks.description:
The `RevalidateEntityButton` component exists but is not imported or used in any entity edit page. Admins cannot trigger per-entity revalidation from the edit UI.

Affected files:
- `apps/admin/src/routes/_authed/accommodations/$id_.edit.tsx`
- `apps/admin/src/routes/_authed/destinations/$id_.edit.tsx`
- `apps/admin/src/routes/_authed/events/$id_.edit.tsx`
- `apps/admin/src/routes/_authed/posts/$id_.edit.tsx`

Key changes: In each edit page, import `RevalidateEntityButton` and render it alongside the save button:
```tsx
<RevalidateEntityButton entityType="accommodation" entityId={id} />
```
Use the correct entityType for each page. The `id` comes from the route params.

Acceptance criteria:
- Button appears in all 4 entity edit pages for users with REVALIDATION_TRIGGER
- Button is hidden for users without the permission (handled by the component)
- Clicking triggers revalidation of that specific entity's paths
- Loading state and success/error toasts work correctly

Resolves: GAP-072, GAP-089, GAP-133.

subtasks.title: Add RevalidateEntityButton to accommodation edit page
Add RevalidateEntityButton to destination edit page
Add RevalidateEntityButton to event edit page
Add RevalidateEntityButton to post edit page

tasks.title: Migrate destinos/index.astro from SSG to SSR
tasks.description:
The destinations listing page `apps/web/src/pages/[lang]/destinos/index.astro` uses `prerender = true` and `getStaticPaths()`, making it a fully static page. New destinations don't appear until the next build. The spec requires this page to be SSR like the other listing pages.

Affected file: `apps/web/src/pages/[lang]/destinos/index.astro`.

Key changes:
- Remove `export const prerender = true`
- Remove `getStaticPaths()` function
- Add dynamic locale validation (follow the pattern of the other 9 migrated pages)
- Replace static params with dynamic `Astro.params.lang` lookup
- Fetch data server-side using the existing API client pattern

Acceptance criteria:
- Page renders SSR on every request
- Locale is validated dynamically
- New destinations appear without a build
- Page builds without TypeScript errors

Resolves: GAP-042, GAP-094.

subtasks.title: Remove prerender=true and getStaticPaths
Add dynamic locale validation following existing SSR page pattern
Replace static data fetch with server-side fetch

tasks.title: Add path and date filters to GET /logs endpoint and model query
tasks.description:
The GET /revalidation/logs endpoint defines `path`, `fromDate`, and `toDate` in its Zod schema but the handler does not forward these filters to the model query. Date filtering is completely non-functional.

Affected files:
- `apps/api/src/routes/revalidation/index.ts`: logs handler (forward filters to model)
- `packages/db/src/models/revalidation-log.model.ts`: add WHERE clauses for path LIKE and date range

Key changes:
- In the handler, extract `path`, `fromDate`, `toDate` from query params and pass to model
- In the model's `findMany`/`findFiltered` method, add `AND path LIKE $path` and `AND created_at BETWEEN $from AND $to` where provided

This is a prerequisite for T-024 (admin logs tab UI).

Acceptance criteria:
- GET /logs?path=/alojamientos returns only logs matching that path
- GET /logs?fromDate=2026-01-01 returns logs from that date forward
- Date range filtering works correctly
- Existing tests pass

Resolves: GAP-022, GAP-051.

tasks.title: Admin logs tab: add filters UI, pagination, sorting and auto-refresh
tasks.description:
The admin revalidation logs tab renders a flat list with no filters, no pagination, no sorting, and no auto-refresh. With real usage volume this page becomes unusable.

Affected files:
- `apps/admin/src/routes/_authed/revalidation/index.tsx` (LogsTab component)
- `apps/admin/src/lib/revalidation-http-adapter/index.ts` (add filter params)
- `apps/admin/src/hooks/useRevalidation.ts` (update logs query to pass filters)

Key changes:
1. HTTP adapter: add filter params to `getRevalidationLogs(filters)` method
2. Logs hook: pass filter state to HTTP adapter call; add `refetchInterval: 30000`
3. Admin page - LogsTab:
   - Add filter controls: Select for entityType, Select for trigger, Select for status, date range inputs for fromDate/toDate
   - Integrate TanStack Table with sorting on date/status/entityType columns
   - Add pagination controls using the page/pageSize params
   - Add `refetchInterval: 30000` for auto-refresh
4. Fix log status badge to use i18n keys instead of raw value (resolves GAP-056)

Acceptance criteria:
- Filters UI: entityType, trigger, status, date range
- Pagination with configurable pageSize
- Sortable columns
- Auto-refresh every 30 seconds
- Status badge shows translated text

Resolves: GAP-019, GAP-022 UI part, GAP-052, GAP-056, GAP-086.

subtasks.title: Add filter params to HTTP adapter getRevalidationLogs method
Update logs hook with filter state and refetchInterval
Add filter controls UI in LogsTab
Integrate TanStack Table with sorting and pagination
Fix status badge to use i18n translation

tasks.title: Add entity-type revalidation mode to admin Manual tab with confirmation dialog
tasks.description:
The admin Manual tab only supports path-based revalidation. The spec requires a second mode: entity-type revalidation with a dropdown of entity types and a confirmation dialog.

Affected file: `apps/admin/src/routes/_authed/revalidation/index.tsx` (ManualTab component).

Key changes:
- Add a second form/section below the existing path textarea
- Include a `<Select>` with all 8 entity types (using i18n keys for display names)
- Add a 'Regenerate all' button that opens an AlertDialog: 'This will regenerate all pages of type [X]. Continue?'
- On confirmation, call POST /revalidate/type via the HTTP adapter's existing `revalidateByType` method (or add it if missing per GAP-061)
- Show success/error toast with count of pages revalidated

Acceptance criteria:
- Entity type dropdown shows all 8 types with translated names
- Confirmation dialog shows before triggering
- POST /revalidate/type is called with selected entity type
- Response shows count of revalidated paths
- Only visible to users with REVALIDATION_TRIGGER permission

Resolves: GAP-025 manual UI part, GAP-071, GAP-087. Also adds missing `revalidateByType` and `checkServiceHealth` to HTTP adapter (GAP-061).

subtasks.title: Add revalidateByType and checkServiceHealth to admin HTTP adapter (GAP-061)
Add entity type Select component in ManualTab
Add AlertDialog confirmation before triggering
Wire to POST /revalidate/type endpoint

tasks.title: Add accessibility aria-labels to admin revalidation UI components
tasks.description:
The admin revalidation page has accessibility gaps: switches lack contextual aria-labels and status badges are not announced to screen readers.

Affected file: `apps/admin/src/routes/_authed/revalidation/index.tsx`.

Key changes:
- Add `aria-label={t('revalidation.config.enabledFor', { entityType })}` to each enable/disable switch, making the label contextual with the entity type name
- Add `role="status"` and `aria-label={t('revalidation.status.' + status)}` to status/trigger badges
- Add `role="status"` and `aria-live="polite"` to InlineNumberField when in edit mode

Acceptance criteria:
- Screen readers announce the entity type name when focus moves to a switch
- Badge elements have accessible role and label
- Edit mode number field announces changes
- No new linting errors

Resolves: GAP-026, GAP-036, GAP-100.

subtasks.title: Add aria-label with entity type to switches
Add role/aria-label to status and trigger badges
Add aria-live to InlineNumberField in edit mode

tasks.title: Write all test gaps: API 401/403, adapter edge cases, E2E Playwright, admin UI tests
tasks.description:
Multiple test coverage gaps exist across the implementation. This task consolidates all remaining test work.

Affected files (test additions):
- `apps/api/test/routes/revalidation/` — API route tests
- `packages/service-core/test/revalidation/adapters.test.ts` — adapter edge cases
- `packages/service-core/test/revalidation/service-hooks.test.ts` — missing 4 services
- `apps/admin/test/` or adjacent — admin UI hook and component tests
- `apps/web/test/revalidation.e2e.test.ts` — Playwright E2E
- Minor gaps: config cache TTL, revalidateMany([]), first-run cron

Key test additions:
1. **API route tests (GAP-030)**: For each of the 8 revalidation endpoints, add explicit tests for: no auth token → 401, valid token + no REVALIDATION_* permission → 403.
2. **Adapter edge cases (GAP-074)**: Add test cases in `adapters.test.ts` for: AbortController timeout (request hangs), HTTP 429 response (rate limit), partial success in revalidateMany (some 200, some 404/500), HTTP 403 auth failure, HTTP 503 service down.
3. **Service hooks — 4 missing services (GAP-059/138)**: Add test suites for PostService hooks (verifying tagSlugs context), AccommodationReviewService (verifying accommodationSlug resolution), DestinationReviewService (verifying destinationSlug and non-empty paths), AmenityService hooks.
4. **Admin UI tests (GAP-034)**: Unit tests for `useRevalidation` hooks (mock API responses), `RevalidateEntityButton` component (permission-hidden state, loading state, success/error toasts), admin HTTP adapter methods.
5. **E2E Playwright (GAP-025)**: Test flow: login as admin → navigate to /revalidation → submit manual revalidation → verify response → check logs tab shows new entry.
6. **Minor test gaps (GAP-139/140/141)**: Config cache TTL expiration test (mock time, verify cache miss after 60s), `revalidateMany([])` empty array guard, first-ever cron run with no prior logs.

Acceptance criteria:
- All 8 API endpoints have 401/403 test coverage
- Adapter tests cover timeout, rate-limit, partial-success, auth-failure
- All 8 services have hook test coverage
- Admin UI components have unit tests
- E2E Playwright test passes in CI
- Minor edge cases are covered

Resolves: GAP-025, GAP-030, GAP-031, GAP-034, GAP-059, GAP-062 (covered by T-001), GAP-074, GAP-138, GAP-139, GAP-140, GAP-141.

subtasks.title: Add 401/403 tests for all 8 API revalidation endpoints

Create E2E Playwright test for manual revalidation flow
Add minor edge case tests (TTL, empty array, first-run cron)

tasks.title: Document API URL divergence and other minor doc/code fixes
tasks.description:
Several minor documentation and code quality issues remain after the implementation phases. This task consolidates them.

Key changes:
1. **CLAUDE.md env var name (GAP-058)**: Fix `apps/web/CLAUDE.md` to replace all references to `HOSPEDA_ISR_BYPASS_TOKEN` with `HOSPEDA_REVALIDATION_SECRET`.
2. **JSDoc in revalidation-init.ts (GAP-069)**: Fix JSDoc on line 15 of `packages/service-core/src/revalidation/revalidation-init.ts` to reference `HOSPEDA_REVALIDATION_SECRET` instead of `HOSPEDA_ISR_BYPASS_TOKEN`.
3. **API URL divergence (from T-009, GAP-049/050/132)**: Confirm the technical decision note was added to the spec/docs.
4. **Astro config limitation comment (GAP-130)**: Add comment in `apps/web/astro.config.mjs` near the `process.env.HOSPEDA_REVALIDATION_SECRET` read explaining why it cannot use the validated env schema.
5. **Verify PT revalidation locale (GAP-136)**: Confirm existence of `packages/i18n/src/locales/pt/revalidation.json`. If missing, create it by translating the 87 keys from en/revalidation.json.

Acceptance criteria:
- No stale env var name in any documentation file
- PT locale file exists with all 87 revalidation keys
- astro.config.mjs has explanatory comment for direct env read
- All docs are consistent with the implementation

Resolves: GAP-058, GAP-069, GAP-130, GAP-136 verification.
